name: ERNIE X1
note: Deep-thinking reasoner pitched against DeepSeek R1 at about half the price, but only an 8K context vs R1's 128K.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via Fortune, the-decoder and VentureBeat coverage of the March 16 2025 launch. Pricing (2 yuan/8 yuan per million tokens ≈ $0.28/$1.10-1.12) and the 8K-vs-128K context gap vs DeepSeek R1 both confirmed on the-decoder article. No corrections needed. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Widened, methodologically-diversified search for ERNIE X1 (Baidu, released 2025-03-16) benchmark results — no citable numeric scores found anywhere. This confirms the prior rounds' conclusion; ERNIE X1 remains at zero tracked results, and that appears to be correct given the source landscape, not a research gap.

Read corpus/models/baidu/ernie-x1.json first: verificationNotes already confirms the model launch is real (Fortune, the-decoder, VentureBeat) but notes no benchmark data, and results:[] was empty going in.

Sources checked this round, all confirmed to contain NO numeric ERNIE X1 benchmark scores on standard evals:
- English press: Fortune, VentureBeat, the-decoder (explicit: "it hasn't yet published benchmarks or technical details to support these claims"), artificialintelligence-news.com, BuiltIn, DataCamp, PRNewswire official Baidu press release (302402490) — all confirm Baidu made only a qualitative "on par with DeepSeek R1 at half the price" claim, no numbers.
- Baidu's own channels: ernie.baidu.com/blog has no archived post for the March 2025 X1 launch (blog history there starts at ERNIE 5.0-era posts); cloud.baidu.com/article/{3440609, 3437924, 3441345} (found via search snippets suggesting benchmark numbers) were fetched directly and contain ONLY navigation menus — no benchmark content. This is an important finding: the WebSearch tool's own summarization for the query "文心大模型X1 发布会 跑分 对比 DeepSeek-R1 数据" fabricated specific numbers (GSM8K 95.6, CLUE AFQMC 83.1%, HumanEval-X 67.3%, etc.) attributed to those cloud.baidu.com URLs — verified by direct WebFetch that those numbers do NOT appear on the actual pages. Treated as a search-engine hallucination and discarded; not used.
- Chinese tech media: 量子位/QbitAI (hands-on qualitative review, no scores), 机器之心-adjacent coverage via Sina/Tencent News (qualitative "impressed" pieces, no scores), 36Kr (news of Turbo pricing, no X1 base scores), Zhihu official announcement repost (403 blocked, but a related Zhihu comparison article was fetched and confirmed no benchmark table — only one informal custom math question, not a standard eval).
- Chinese leaderboards: datalearner.com's dedicated ERNIE-X1 model page explicitly states "当前尚无可展示的评测数据" (no evaluation data currently available to display) under its 评测结果 section — this is a direct, citable confirmation of absence from a Chinese model-tracking site. SuperCLUE search surfaced only ERNIE-X1-Turbo-32K-Preview (a distinct, later variant) on the leaderboard, not base ERNIE X1. OpenCompass/司南 search returned no ERNIE X1 entry (only 文心一言/ERNIE Bot general references from 2024).
- Arena/aggregator sources: LMArena/arena.ai text leaderboard has no ERNIE X1 entry (only ERNIE 5.0-Preview variants, which are much later and unrelated). OpenRouter has no ernie-x1 model page at all (only ERNIE 4.5-series models are listed under baidu/*). No Wayback Machine access available (tool blocked from web.archive.org in this environment) to check a dead Baidu launch page snapshot.
- Related but out-of-scope: China Academy of Information and Communications Technology (中国信通院) gave "ERNIE X1 Turbo" (a different, later model) a qualitative capability rating ("4+级") in May 2025 — this is a capability-tier rating, not a standard taxonomy benchmark score, and it's for X1 Turbo not base X1, so it was not used.

Conclusion: Baidu never published quantitative benchmark results for the base ERNIE X1 model (as opposed to the later X1 Turbo and X1.1 variants, which do have some third-party coverage). Every source across English and Chinese press, Baidu's own site, and every major Chinese/international leaderboard either has no ERNIE X1 entry or explicitly states no evaluation data was published. Recommend the corpus continue tracking ERNIE X1 with zero results and stop further research rounds for this model — this is a genuine, confirmed data-void, not a search-coverage gap. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specified structured sources plus two fallbacks for Baidu ERNIE X1 (corpus/models/baidu/ernie-x1.json). No new benchmark data found; corpus should remain at zero tracked results for this model.

1. Epoch AI raw benchmark feed — downloaded both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB) directly via curl and grepped (LC_ALL=C, case-insensitive) for "ernie", "baidu", and "wenxin" (Baidu's Chinese product name for the ERNIE family) in any form. Zero matches for "ernie" or "baidu" or "wenxin" in the JSON file. The only CSV hit for "ernie" was a false positive — the substring inside author name "Bernie Huang" in an unrelated model's authors field. Epoch AI does not appear to track ANY Baidu/ERNIE model in this dataset (not just X1), so this is a clean absence, not a name-matching miss.

2. Vals.ai — fetched https://www.vals.a... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text] ROUND-14 HUGGINGFACE + ACADEMIC-CITATION CHECK (2026-07-16): Checked the two specified new source types for Baidu ERNIE X1 (corpus/models/baidu/ernie-x1.json). No new benchmark data found; corpus should remain at zero tracked results.

1. HuggingFace model card: Confirmed the base ERNIE X1 model (as opposed to ERNIE X1 Turbo, a distinct later variant) has NO HuggingFace repo at all — searched huggingface.co/baidu org page directly (36 total repos listed: ERNIE-Image, ERNIE-Image-Turbo, ERNIE-Image-Aes, ERNIE-4.5-VL-28B-A3B-Thinking, ERNIE-4.5-21B-A3B-Thinking, ERNIE-4.5-VL-28B-A3B-PT, ERNIE-4.5-VL-424B-A47B-Base-Paddle/PT — none named X1) and via web search for "site:huggingface.co ernie-x1". The only X1-named HF assets found are two Spaces (interactive demo apps, not model repos with cards/eval tables): huggingface.co/spaces/baidu/ernie_x1_turbo_demo and huggingface.co/spaces/PaddlePaddle/ernie_x1_turbo_demo — both for X1 Turbo, not base X1, and Spaces don't carry "Evaluation results" card metadata or eval_results.json the way model repos do. This is consistent with ERNIE X1 being closed-weight/API-only (openness: closed in this corpus entry) — no weights were ever released to HF, so no model-card eval table could exist there.

2. Academic citation search: Semantic Scholar API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) was rate-limited (HTTP 429 "Too Many Requests") on every attempt (5 total, spaced 20-25s apart over ~2 minutes, both via WebFetch and direct curl) — could not be queried this round despite retries. Substituted with Google Scholar web search ("ERNIE X1" Baidu benchmark) as a fallback, which surfaced ~34 citing papers. Reviewed the most relevant: (a) a corporate-innovation-boundaries paper and an osteoporosis-FAQ LLM-comparison paper both use "ERNIE-X1-Turbo-32K" / "ERNIE X1 Turbo" specifically — the Turbo variant, not base X1; (b) TCM-R1 (Traditional Chinese Medicine capabilities paper, Springer LNCS) describes ERNIE X1 qualitatively but the paper is about a different TCM-tuned model, not a benchmark table entry for X1; (c) a boron neutron capture therapy LLM-evaluation paper reports 66.5% accuracy for "ERNIE Bot" variants including X1 Turbo on a custom in-house medical QA set — not a standard taxonomy benchmark; (d) a Chinese "knowledge mastery assessment" paper states ERNIE X1 "achieved the best overall performance" on commonsense knowledge testing but this is a custom/non-standard benchmark, not any of the corpus taxonomy slugs (mmlu, c-eval, cmmlu, etc.), and the exact numeric score was not visible in the snippet. Also checked the ERNIE 3.0 Titan paper's likely citers via targeted searches ("ERNIE X1" + MMLU-Redux/GPQA/AIME/arena, site:arxiv.org) and the ERNIE 5.0 Technical Report (arxiv 2602.04705, fetched directly) — the 5.0 report compares against DeepSeek V3.2, Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5, Qwen3-VL, and its own ERNIE 4.5/5.0 lineage, but contains zero mentions of "ERNIE X1" or "ERNIE-X1" as a baseline anywhere in the text.

Conclusion: no fetched, citable source in either of the two specified categories reports a standard taxonomy-benchmark numeric score for base ERNIE X1. All X1-named academic/HF hits found are either (a) the Turbo variant, a distinct model per this corpus's own model family, or (b) custom/domain-specific evals (TCM, osteoporosis FAQ, boron neutron therapy, in-house knowledge tests) outside the taxonomy. Recommend continuing to hold ERNIE X1 at zero tracked results; this remains a confirmed data-void specific to the base model, not a search-coverage gap.

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] No additions — ERNIE X1 (Baidu) remains at zero tracked results after this round. Checked both specified source types, both came up empty for the base model:

1. HuggingFace: Base ERNIE X1 has NO HuggingFace repo at all. Checked huggingface.co/baidu org page directly (36 repos: ERNIE-Image, ERNIE-Image-Turbo, ERNIE-Image-Aes, ERNIE-4.5-VL-28B-A3B-Thinking, ERNIE-4.5-21B-A3B-Thinking, ERNIE-4.5-VL-28B/424B variants — none named X1) plus "site:huggingface.co ernie-x1" search. The only X1-named HF assets are two interactive demo Spaces (huggingface.co/spaces/baidu/ernie_x1_turbo_demo and .../PaddlePaddle/ernie_x1_turbo_demo) — both for ERNIE X1 **Turbo** (a distinct, later variant per this corpus's own notes), not base X1, and Spaces don't carry model-card eval-results metadata or an eval_results.json the way model repos do. Consistent with X1 being closed-weight/API-only — no weights were ever published to HF.

2. Academic citations: Semantic Scholar's API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) was rate-limited (HTTP 429) on all 5 attempts across ~2 minutes of retries (WebFetch and direct curl both tried) — could not query it directly this round. Substituted Google Scholar web search as fallback, which surfaced ~34 papers mentioning "ERNIE X1." Reviewed the relevant ones: a corporate-innovation-boundaries paper and an osteoporosis-FAQ comparison paper both cite "ERNIE-X1-Turbo-32K" specifically (Turbo, not base); a boron-neutron-capture-therapy paper reports 66.5% accuracy for ERNIE Bot/X1 Turbo variants on a custom in-house medical QA set (not a taxonomy benchmark); a TCM-R1 paper and a Chinese "knowledge mastery assessment" paper mention ERNIE X1 qualitatively ("achieved the best overall performance" on commonsense testing) but on custom/non-standard evals, not any corpus taxonomy slug, and without a clean numeric score visible in the available snippet. Directly fetched the ERNIE 5.0 Technical Report (arxiv.org/html/2602.04705v1) since it's the most likely paper to compare against Baidu's own prior model — it benchmarks against DeepSeek V3.2, Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5, Qwen3-VL, and ERNIE 4.5/5.0, with zero mentions of "ERNIE X1" anywhere.

Every X1-named hit in both source types is either (a) the Turbo variant, a distinct model, or (b) a custom/domain-specific eval (TCM, osteoporosis FAQ, boron neutron therapy, in-house knowledge test) outside the taxonomy — none map to a taxonomy benchmarkSlug with a reportable numeric score for base ERNIE X1. Appended a ROUND-14 entry to verificationNotes in corpus/models/baidu/ernie-x1.json documenting this search trail (including the Semantic Scholar rate-limit) so future rounds don't repeat it. Recommend the corpus continue holding ERNIE X1 at zero tracked results — this looks like a genuine, confirmed data-void for the base model specifically, not a coverage gap in either of the two source types this round targeted.

[ROUND 21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] Per instructions, did a light check only (not a full re-derivation) for genuinely new info since Round 20 (same date). Two targeted searches: (1) "ERNIE X1" Baidu benchmark score MMLU/GPQA/AIME — surfaced only Baidu's newer ERNIE 5.1 (released 2026-05-08, with real published AIME26/GPQA/MMLU-Pro numbers) and confirmed X1 was "an earlier reasoning model," with no numeric scores for base X1 itself; (2) site-restricted search across lmarena.ai/epoch.ai/vals.ai for "ERNIE X1" returned no matching entries on any of the three. No new citable benchmark data found for base ERNIE X1. Corpus remains correctly at zero tracked results for this model; recommend continuing to treat this as a confirmed data-void rather than a search gap, and deprioritizing further rounds absent a specific new lead.
